title: Java Module Declaration
description: Declare a named module with requires and exports directives.
learningGoals: Declare a named module and its dependencies in module-info.java.
Contrast requires transitive with a qualified exports ... to clause.

completionChecks.prompt: What does requires transitive java.sql do?
choices: Makes java.sql readable to modules that read this module
Exports every package in this module
completionChecks.explanation: requires transitive re-exports readability so downstream modules that require this module also read java.sql.

completionChecks.prompt: What is special about exports com.example.app.spi to com.example.plugin?
choices: It exports the package to all modules
It is a qualified export to one named module
It imports that package from com.example.plugin
completionChecks.explanation: The to clause makes the export qualified, so only the listed module receives that exported package.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens if another module requires com.example.app and uses java.sql.Connection?
choices: It compiles because java.sql readability is re-exported by requires transitive
It fails unless that module also declares requires java.sql
It compiles only when the other module is named com.example.plugin
It fails because exports controls readability of required modules
completionChecks.explanation: requires transitive affects downstream readability; the qualified export line is a separate package-access rule.
